title: Pleasantly Gooey Vietnamese Red Bean Paste Bun Bo Hue
description: A malty and gooey solo dinner Vietnamese dish featuring anko red bean paste, this recipe is a FODMAP friendly version of the classic Bun Bo Hue, adapted to be safe for sensitive stomachs.
instructions: Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions and set aside.
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and add the sliced onion, cooking until translucent.
Add the pork sausage to the pan and cook until browned, breaking up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the garlic-infused oil, fish sauce, and lime juice to the pan, stirring to combine.
Stir in the red bean paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, until the mixture is heated through and starting to caramelize.
Add the cooked noodles to the pan, tossing to combine with the sauce and sausage mixture.
Top the noodles with bean sprouts, scallions, and a sprinkle of salt.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional scallions and lime wedges if desired.
To achieve the pleasantly gooey texture, cook the red bean paste mixture over low heat for an additional 5 minutes, stirring constantly, before adding the noodles to the pan.
